text: Writing clean code is essential for any developer who wants to work efficiently. The most important goal is to make your code readable for yourself and others. Good naming conventions help with this, as variable and function names should clearly describe what they do. Instead of using a short, unclear name like "a", use a descriptive one like "totalPrice". Functions should be small and focus on a single task. This makes them easier to test and reuse. Ultimately, clean code improves maintainability, meaning it is simpler to update, fix bugs, or add new features later. A little extra effort now saves a lot of time in the future.
